title: TopBuild Q4 2025 Earnings Call Highlights - Management Guidance and Outlook
text:
TopBuild Corp. Q4 2025 Earnings Call and Management Commentary:

2026 Full Year Guidance:
For 2026, TopBuild guided to revenue between $5.925 billion and $6.225 billion, with a midpoint of $6.075 billion, and adjusted EBITDA of $1.005 billion to $1.155 billion with a midpoint of $1.08 billion.

Segment Outlook:
The forecast assumes low-single-digit declines in both price and volume, a mid-single-digit drop in residential, low-single-digit growth in commercial and industrial, and M&A contributing $800 million to $850 million in sales. This reflects management's cautious stance on near-term macro conditions.

Margin Expectations:
EBITDA margins are expected to range from 16.5% to 18.5% by quarter. For 2026, TopBuild has embedded roughly $55 million of price/cost headwinds into its outlook, acknowledging likely margin drag from competitive pressures.

Management Tone:
Management highlighted strong cash generation and a decade-long record of growth, yet acknowledged meaningful organic volume declines and margin pressure. Executives stressed cost discipline and synergy capture, but their tone on near-term housing demand and pricing was notably guarded.

Operational Priorities:
Management emphasized acquisitions, cost discipline, and synergy realization as key drivers in a challenging macro environment.
